response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong track record in web platforms, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but his background has virtually no overlap with the core requirements of this role. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, GPU architecture, hardware-software co-design, SIMD/MIMD expertise, or deep-learning workload optimization — all of which are central to the Principal Performance Architect position. His current AI-adjacent work involves agentic workflow infrastructure at the application layer, not hardware-level AI accelerator optimization. The role demands a PhD/Master's-level hardware or computer architecture background; his CS Bachelor's alone does not disqualify him, but the absence of any chip design, microarchitecture, or ML systems experience across 13+ years makes him a poor fit.
strengths: 13+ years of software engineering experience across diverse domains including low-latency systems and embedded work
Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and caching
Current exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and tooling, showing awareness of the AI domain
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or any hardware-level performance analysis relevant to AI accelerators
Entire career is software-layer focused (web, mobile, backend, tooling) with no hardware-software co-design or chip architecture work
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization, which are explicitly preferred qualifications

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU workload expertise
